gap: Gradle project with Kotlin (Quarkus + Kotlin 2.0.21). Agent set compileJava target to 25 but compileKotlin stayed at 21 -> Gradle Kotlin plugin fails: 'Inconsistent JVM-target compatibility detected for tasks compileJava (25) and compileKotlin (21)'. Two-layer wall: (1) JVM-target alignment - compileKotlin must match compileJava; prefer kotlin{jvmToolchain(25)}. (2) Kotlin 2.0.21 cannot emit JVM target 25 (needs >=2.2), and the Kotlin version is pinned by the Quarkus platform BOM, so a raw Kotlin bump is overridden - must bump the Quarkus platform. Agent attempted Kotlin 2.0.21->2.2.0/2.4.0 and JvmTarget.JVM_25 edits; none stuck (BOM override). SKILL.md section G (Gradle track) covers only Java-only Gradle; no Kotlin-coexistence guidance.
decision: logged-only (operator: do not fix this pass)

gap: Compiles clean under 25; 145/147 tests pass. The 2 lost tests use EasyMock.createMock(), which builds proxies via ByteBuddy ClassInjector$UsingLookup. On JDK 25 the project's bundled ByteBuddy is too old: 'Could not create type ... CloudletScheduler$$$EasyMock$1 must be defined in the same package as org.easymock.internal.ClassProxyFactory'. Same family as the Mockito/ByteBuddy 'unsupported class file major version' wall, via EasyMock. Deterministic fix: bump net.bytebuddy:byte-buddy + byte-buddy-agent (and/or EasyMock >=5.5) to a JDK-25-aware version. Agent left the test-mocking deps untouched.
fixability: high (pure dependency-version floor, no BOM conflict)
decision: logged-only (operator: do not fix this pass)

gap: Spring Boot Gradle project; compileJava/compileTestJava all UP-TO-DATE (bump compiled fine). Agent added '--add-opens java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED' to the test task jvmArgs as a SINGLE space-joined token -> test JVM rejects it: 'Unrecognized option: --add-opens ...; Could not create the Java Virtual Machine', killing the only previously-passing test (contextLoads). Correct form is one token with '=' (jvmArgs("--add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED")) or the two-arg form ('--add-opens','java.base/...'). agent.log shows the agent diagnosed the space-vs-'=' problem and even considered removing the flag, but the final build.gradle still had the broken space form. The --add-opens was likely unnecessary for this trivial contextLoads.
skill_gap: §G --add-opens example can be mis-applied (wrong token format); robustness, not a platform wall
fixability: trivial (use '=' token form, or omit the unneeded flag)
decision: logged-only (operator: do not fix this pass)
